source_document.title: Vineyard Workflow Discovery for a Vineyard Information System

claims.title: Seasonal planning, dispatch, execution, and verified closure
claims.claim: Vineyard managers translate seasonal and event-driven priorities into block-level assignments, supervise execution and exceptions, and need attributable completion evidence rather than a flattened done flag.

source_anchor.section_heading: Detailed workflow cards and handoff maps
source_anchor.anchor_text: Workflow card: seasonal work planning and work-order dispatch

claims.limitations: Public sources establish responsibilities and records more strongly than the exact local dispatch choreography.
field_validation_questions: Which role may release blocked work and who verifies closure in each operating organization?

claims.title: Scouting, recommendation, crop protection, and defensible application record
claims.claim: Monitoring and observed pressure lead through treatment selection, safety and restriction checks, execution, application recording, and efficacy follow-up with block and operator attribution.

source_anchor.section_heading: Detailed workflow cards and handoff maps
source_anchor.anchor_text: Workflow card: scouting to spray execution and compliance record

claims.limitations: Treatment thresholds, approval authority, products, and legal reporting requirements vary by jurisdiction and buyer.
field_validation_questions: Where are scouting observations first captured and which approval or buyer checks delay execution most often?

claims.title: Irrigation scheduling, fertility adjustment, and delivery verification
claims.claim: Water and nutrient decisions combine site conditions, plant observations, weather, tissue results, prior inputs, and block history, then require executable instructions and evidence of actual delivery or malfunction.

source_anchor.section_heading: Detailed workflow cards and handoff maps
source_anchor.anchor_text: Workflow card: irrigation scheduling and fertility adjustment

claims.limitations: Agronomic evidence is strong, while the exact manager-to-irrigator handoff and sensor availability are organization-specific.
field_validation_questions: How is actual irrigation delivery verified when the office plan differs from valve, pump, or field conditions?

claims.title: Crop estimation and forecast revision
claims.claim: Representative vine and cluster sampling produces a revisable block forecast that informs crop adjustment, winery capacity, labor, container planning, and budget expectations through harvest.

source_anchor.section_heading: Detailed workflow cards and handoff maps
source_anchor.anchor_text: Workflow card: crop estimation and maturity tracking to harvest-date decision

claims.limitations: Sampling methods, forecast tolerances, and revision cadence vary by block, variety, contract, and organization.
field_validation_questions: Which revisions trigger downstream labor, tank, contract, or financial replanning and who is notified?

claims.title: Maturity sampling, winery communication, and pick decision
claims.claim: Block-specific representative samples, laboratory values, sensory and condition observations, weather, capacity, and contract authority converge into an attributable pick-date decision with increasing sampling cadence near harvest.

source_anchor.section_heading: Detailed workflow cards and handoff maps
source_anchor.anchor_text: Workflow card: crop estimation and maturity tracking to harvest-date decision

claims.limitations: Final pick authority and acceptable quality thresholds are contract- and winery-specific.
field_validation_questions: How are conflicting field and winery samples reconciled and where is final pick authority recorded?

claims.title: Harvest, load identity, intake quality, and reconciliation
claims.claim: A final pick authorization becomes coordinated crews, containers, transport, block-identified loads, intake weights and quality results, acceptance or rejection decisions, corrected tickets, and settlement inputs.

source_anchor.section_heading: Detailed workflow cards and handoff maps
source_anchor.anchor_text: Workflow card: harvest execution to weigh-ticket and payment reconciliation

claims.limitations: Commercial tolerances, rejection remedies, pricing, and settlement rules differ by contract and jurisdiction.
field_validation_questions: Which identifiers and signatures survive from pick authorization through corrected weigh ticket and final settlement?

claims.title: Crew scheduling, attendance, time, payroll, and cost allocation
claims.claim: Field work planning depends on labor availability and accountable crew supervision, while completed activity quantities and time must later support payroll, costing, budgets, and management reporting.

source_anchor.anchor_text: Parallel to all of this, office functions handle payroll, purchasing, invoice matching, certifications, insurance, and management reporting.

claims.limitations: Public evidence supports the responsibility chain but not the frequency or exact mechanics of attendance and payroll correction.
field_validation_questions: How often are time, activity, block allocation, or pay details corrected after field submission and by whom?

claims.title: Materials, purchasing, equipment readiness, and repair
claims.claim: Seasonal and urgent work is executable only when materials, compliant products, calibrated equipment, maintenance status, labor, and timing are coordinated, with exceptions preserved when readiness fails.

source_anchor.anchor_text: Keep work, materials, equipment, and paperwork synchronized.

claims.limitations: Inventory policy, purchasing authority, service intervals, and repair evidence vary widely by operator.
field_validation_questions: Which readiness checks cause the most late replanning and how are temporary repairs or substitutions approved?

claims.title: Grower onboarding and contract-fruit coordination
claims.claim: Contract fruit adds a cross-organization relationship loop linking identities, maps, contracts, quality expectations, spray history, crop estimates, maturity results, harvest schedules, delivery outcomes, and disputes.

source_anchor.anchor_text: Contract fruit adds a relationship-management loop

claims.limitations: Contract language, final authority, communication norms, and grower data maturity vary materially.
field_validation_questions: What must be complete before a grower or block is harvest-ready, and how are conflicting records resolved across organizations?

claims.title: Certification, annual evidence, audit, and corrective action
claims.claim: Certification turns ordinary field records into annual submissions, self-assessments, block-level proof, independent audit material, findings, timed corrective actions, and renewal decisions.

source_anchor.section_heading: Certification overlays, exception inventory, digital opportunity map, field research guide, and sources
source_anchor.anchor_text: Certified-organic workflows do not merely change field practices

claims.limitations: Program rules and evidence cadence differ; the atlas must label the active standard and vintage rather than universalize controls.
field_validation_questions: Which evidence gaps most often become corrective actions, and which source record is least trusted during audit preparation?

claims.title: Stable block identity across organizations and history
claims.claim: Row and block identity is a foundational interoperability concern because operational units are renamed, split, combined, and represented differently across vineyard, grower, winery, compliance, and accounting systems.

source_anchor.anchor_text: the same block is named differently across grower, vineyard, winery, compliance, and accounting systems

claims.limitations: A universal industry identifier is not assumed; the product preserves stable internal identity plus source-system aliases and effective dates.
field_validation_questions: Which split, merge, rename, and alias events must remain effective-dated to reconcile historical records correctly?

claims.title: Seasonal hazards, urgent replanning, and immutable exception history
claims.claim: Frost, heat, smoke, weather, worker restrictions, equipment failure, winery delay, quality rejection, and missing records can override the seasonal plan and require urgent decisions, escalation, recovery, and preserved corrections.

source_anchor.anchor_text: Event-driven exceptions can override the calendar.

claims.limitations: Trigger thresholds and response authority are regional and organizational, and smoke evidence may remain inherently uncertain.
field_validation_questions: Which exceptions require immediate stop, approval, external communication, or supersession rather than ordinary rescheduling?
